title: pupalia lappacea var. tomentosa
intro: Pupalia lappacea var. tomentosa, also known as bushy desert-hair or woolly desert-hair, is a plant species that belongs to the family Amaranthaceae. It is traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Pupalia lappacea var. tomentosa include the treatment of wounds, fever reduction, and relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infused oil, or dried powder for its medicinal uses.
